transferable_mechanism: Bayesian count-mixture deconvolution can inspire exploratory transfer to non-Visium spatial assays whose locations remain mixed cell-type signals.
compatibility_gaps: requires matched or defensible single-cell reference; new assay geometry needs validation; abundance estimates cannot be assumed benchmark-backed
risk_notes: Addresses v0.8 false negative MIG_V08_003 and should remain exploratory, not a direct replacement claim.
reviewer_notes: Bayesian count-mixture deconvolution may be explored for non-Visium or pixel-aggregated spatial matrices, but new assay geometry requires explicit validation and no benchmark-backed performance claim is allowed.

transferable_mechanism: Reference-correlation scoring can transfer as an exploratory label-similarity mechanism for cross-tissue atlas problems when no directly matched annotator exists.
compatibility_gaps: requires trusted reference atlas; label hierarchy and domain shift must be checked; unseen states remain weak
risk_notes: Addresses v0.8 reference-label migration gap while preserving reference-coverage caveat.
reviewer_notes: Reference-correlation scoring may serve as a descriptive label-similarity signal across atlases, but it must not be treated as a decisive classifier; unseen cell states and reference coverage remain major caveats.

transferable_mechanism: Reference-trained classifier confidence can transfer as an exploratory label-similarity mechanism for cross-tissue atlas annotation hypotheses.
compatibility_gaps: requires curated reference model; domain shift and label granularity must be validated; classifier confidence is not biological certainty
risk_notes: Companion vector to SingleR for v0.9 reference-label transfer.
reviewer_notes: Classifier log-odds may be reused as an exploratory association score, but classifier confidence is sensitive to domain shift and is not a biological gold standard.

transferable_mechanism: Embedding-level correction can transfer as a fast exploratory cross-sample alignment prototype when raw-count generative modeling is not required.
compatibility_gaps: requires precomputed high-quality embedding; cannot infer raw count corrections; alignment must be validated against biology preservation
risk_notes: Targets embedding-level alignment prototype vectors surfaced in v0.8.
reviewer_notes: Embedding-level soft-cluster correction is acceptable for rapid alignment prototypes only; it cannot restore or generate raw counts and must be blocked for generative count-modeling requests.

transferable_mechanism: Anchor or WNN matching ideas can transfer as exploratory cross-sample or cross-modal alignment hypotheses.
compatibility_gaps: version-specific workflow; claims must specify anchor/WNN scope; not a single fixed algorithmic guarantee
risk_notes: Useful for migration hypotheses only when the mechanism is anchor/WNN matching, not generic Seurat superiority.
reviewer_notes: Accept only when the transferable mechanism is explicitly CCA/RPCA/WNN anchor matching; block generic claims that Seurat itself is broadly superior.

transferable_mechanism: Pretrained cell embeddings can be used as exploratory feature extractors for rare representation-transfer tasks.
compatibility_gaps: independent third-party benchmarks limited; embeddings do not provide causal proof; downstream validation required
risk_notes: Reduces profile-only foundation-model migration paths while preserving benchmark caveat.
reviewer_notes: Pretrained cell embeddings may support exploratory linear probing or feature extraction, but they must not be used to infer causal regulatory networks without separate validation.

transferable_mechanism: Niche-aware embeddings can inspire exploratory spatial microenvironment representation hypotheses.
compatibility_gaps: must not replace deconvolution tools; spatial embedding does not equal abundance estimate; validation required
risk_notes: Accept only for niche representation or mechanism comparison, not spatial deconvolution.
reviewer_notes: Niche-aware spatial transformer attention is accepted only for exploratory microenvironment representation; it must never replace cell2location-style spatial deconvolution or abundance estimation.

transferable_mechanism: Reference-based spatial abundance modeling can serve as a contrastive mechanism when comparing niche representation against dedicated composition models.
compatibility_gaps: only supporting comparator for mechanism analysis; not a foundation model; requires matched reference and deconvolution validation
risk_notes: Supports mechanism-comparison queries where cell2location is an explicit comparator, not a general representation source.
reviewer_notes: cell2location is accepted only as a composition-model comparator or higher-order control baseline in foundation-model evaluation; it is not a foundation representation source.

transferable_mechanism: Transition-kernel composition and absorbing-state probability can transfer as exploratory fate-hypothesis machinery when a credible kernel exists.
compatibility_gaps: requires transition kernel, pseudotime, velocity, or other justified dynamics; UMAP alone is insufficient
risk_notes: Needed for fate-hypothesis positives and boundary cases.
reviewer_notes: Markov absorbing-state probabilities and transition-kernel cascades are valid only when a legitimate transition kernel exists; UMAP-only graphs must be hard-blocked.

transferable_mechanism: Splicing kinetic directionality can transfer only as an exploratory dynamic-state hypothesis when spliced/unspliced layers are present.
compatibility_gaps: requires spliced/unspliced layers; kNN smoothing and kinetic assumptions can distort direction; not valid for ordinary expression-only matrices
risk_notes: Accept as RNA-velocity-specific mechanism with strong layer requirement.
reviewer_notes: scVelo-style ODE directionality is accepted only when explicit spliced/unspliced layers exist; ordinary expression-only count matrices must be hard-blocked.

transferable_mechanism: Artificial-doublet generation can transfer as an exploratory synthetic-positive anomaly scoring mechanism.
compatibility_gaps: requires task-specific synthetic positive generator and threshold validation; not a generic QC detector
risk_notes: Complements Scrublet for simulation-based anomaly migration.
reviewer_notes: Artificial-positive mixture simulation can be explored for anomaly scoring, but it must not be treated as generic QC filtering without a task-specific simulator and threshold validation.

transferable_mechanism: Deep latent-variable modeling can transfer as exploratory probabilistic multi-view representation when uncertainty and covariates matter.
compatibility_gaps: black-box model; requires training diagnostics; not automatically interpretable or benchmark-backed for migration target
risk_notes: Keep conservative unless the query explicitly needs probabilistic latent modeling and validation.
reviewer_notes: Do not promote suite-level scvi-tools migration. Require explicit subclass and loss definition, such as MultiVI or totalVI, before any exploratory vector is accepted.

transferable_mechanism: AnnData-centered workflow utilities may support migration prototypes but do not constitute a single transferable algorithm.
compatibility_gaps: toolkit not a single algorithm; benchmark claims must be function-specific; avoid treating as migration source mechanism
risk_notes: Marking this reviewed should prevent silent profile-only status, but it should not be promoted as accepted migration vector.
reviewer_notes: Reject toolkit-level Scanpy migration: AnnData workflow utilities are not a discrete transferable mathematical algorithm and must not become API-pile graph edges.
